answered.question: Input type (blank DOCX vs existing GW)
answered.answer: Blank client DOCX

answered.question: UI layout (wizard vs single page)
answered.answer: Step-by-step wizard

answered.question: User-provided metadata
answered.answer: Template type + language selection

answered.question: Final output format
answered.answer: Download DOCX only

answered.question: LLM input format
answered.answer: Parsed DOCX structure JSON

answered.question: LLM adaptation passes
answered.answer: Two-pass: analyze then insert

answered.question: Reference template strategy
answered.answer: Include matching reference in prompt

answered.question: Template type detection
answered.answer: User selects, LLM validates

answered.question: Preview content
answered.answer: Rendered with GW dummy data (single local report)

answered.question: Feedback mechanism
answered.answer: Chat-style text input (annotations deferred)

answered.question: Iteration limits
answered.answer: Soft limit with warning after 5

answered.question: Mapping plan editing
answered.answer: Viewable + correctable via chat

answered.question: Placeholder placement strategy
answered.answer: Semantic section matching

answered.question: DOCX modification method
answered.answer: LLM outputs structured instructions, Python applies

answered.question: Rich text markers
answered.answer: Auto-apply based on field type

answered.question: Custom Jinja2 features
answered.answer: Template-type-aware rules engine

answered.question: Template storage
answered.answer: Session-scoped with optional save

answered.question: Template validation
answered.answer: LLM validation in Pass 1

answered.question: GW preview data source
answered.answer: Single local GW dummy report (always Report ID 1)

answered.question: Wizard state persistence
answered.answer: Auto-save to session, resume on return

answered.question: Mapping plan display format
answered.answer: Visual mapping table with confidence

answered.question: LLM error recovery
answered.answer: Checkpoint + retry from last good state

answered.question: Processing visibility
answered.answer: Progress steps with status (no raw streaming)

answered.question: Jinja2 syntax validation
answered.answer: Validate against whitelist before applying to DOCX

answered.question: Wizard step count
answered.answer: 5 steps

answered.question: Unusual template handling
answered.answer: LLM warns + best-effort mapping

answered.question: Formatting preservation
answered.answer: Preserve client formatting exactly

answered.question: Wizard navigation
answered.answer: Back button to any completed step

answered.question: Sidebar placement
answered.answer: Top-level Template Adapter route

answered.question: Download metadata
answered.answer: DOCX only, audit log captures metadata

answered.question: Reference template versioning
answered.answer: SHA-256 hash logged in audit trail

answered.question: Coexistence with Phase 8
answered.answer: Separate routes, shared components

answered.question: Preview highlight rendering method
answered.answer: DOCX paragraph shading via python-docx + Gotenberg pipeline

answered.question: Step 2 layout with annotated preview
answered.answer: Tabbed: Preview tab (annotated PDF) + Mappings tab (editable table), Chat stays right, default to Preview

answered.question: When to persist mappings to knowledge base
answered.answer: After successful download (Step 5 completion)

answered.question: Few-shot injection count and selection
answered.answer: 3-5 examples ranked by usage_count, filtered by template_type + language

answered.question: Knowledge base scope (per-user vs shared)
answered.answer: Shared across all users

answered.question: Gap detection method
answered.answer: Compare mapping plan against reference template's known GW fields

answered.question: Section text matching strategy for knowledge base
answered.answer: Normalized text patterns (lowercase, strip whitespace, remove client-specific words)

answered.question: PDF highlight interactivity
answered.answer: Read-only with tooltips; user marks missing sections via paragraph picker on Mappings tab

answered.question: Knowledge base admin UI
answered.answer: No admin UI for now (defer until needed)

answered.question: Minimum confidence threshold for knowledge base persistence
answered.answer: No threshold — save all confirmed mappings (download = approval)

answered.question: Gap correction method
answered.answer: Inline edit on table rows (click GW Field cell, type correct field)

answered.question: Knowledge base learning visibility
answered.answer: Silent few-shot — LLM learns automatically, no badges

answered.question: Training mode vs normal flow
answered.answer: Normal wizard flow each time (knowledge base grows organically)

answered.question: Missing section selection method
answered.answer: Paragraph picker list (scrollable list of unmapped DOCX paragraphs)

answered.question: Paragraph picker placement
answered.answer: '+ Add Missing Section' button below mapping table, opens searchable popover

answered.question: When should translation happen in the wizard flow?
answered.answer: New optional wizard step after preview approval

answered.question: How should Jinja2 variables be preserved during translation?
answered.answer: Placeholder replacement (replace with numbered tokens, translate, restore)

answered.question: How should European Portuguese (PT-PT) quality be enforced?
answered.answer: Strong PT-PT instructions in prompt + curated pentest glossary (10-20 terms)

answered.question: What text should be translated in the template?
answered.answer: All static text (everything except Jinja2 expressions)

answered.question: How should the translation direction be determined?
answered.answer: Auto-detect from source language (EN→PT-PT or PT-PT→EN)

answered.question: What should the user be able to download after translation?
answered.answer: Both versions (original adapted + translated) as separate DOCX files

answered.question: Which LLM model should handle translation?
answered.answer: Sonnet 4.5 via per-feature config (upgradeable to Opus via config)

answered.question: Should the LLM see a reference template in the target language?
answered.answer: Yes, include target language reference for terminology/style context

answered.question: How should translation review work (given Phase 5.1 layout)?
answered.answer: Preview + Chat only (no Mappings tab), consistent with Phase 5.1 correction pattern

answered.question: How should translation progress be displayed?
answered.answer: Progress steps (Extract tokens → Translate → Restore Jinja2 → Validate → Preview)

answered.question: What happens to translation if original adapted template is edited?
answered.answer: Translation invalidated, manual re-trigger required

answered.question: How should text selection work on the PDF?
answered.answer: Select + auto-add — highlight text, immediately added as next numbered selection with toast confirmation

answered.question: Where should the document structure browser be placed?
answered.answer: Collapsible sidebar in PDF viewer — toggle button on toolbar opens sidebar overlay listing all paragraphs

answered.question: How should the batch chat message work when describing multiple selections?
answered.answer: Free-form natural language — user types '#1 is X, #2 is Y', LLM parses references

answered.question: How should the LLM's mapping results be presented for user confirmation?
answered.answer: Inline on PDF with accept/reject per mapping — overlays at selection locations, batch confirm-all button

answered.question: How should coverage progress and KB activity be shown to the user?
answered.answer: Minimal counter + subtle KB badge — 'X mapped' toolbar counter, brain icon +1 animation

answered.question: When the user rejects a mapping, what should happen next?
answered.answer: Selection stays numbered, turns orange; user re-describes in chat, LLM re-maps only specified selections

answered.question: Should the user be able to correct multiple rejected mappings in one chat message?
answered.answer: Yes, same batch pattern — multiple corrections in one message, consistent with initial batch-describe flow

answered.question: How should rejected selections be visually distinguished?
answered.answer: Three states: blue outline (pending), green fill (confirmed), orange outline (rejected/needs correction)

answered.question: How should the initial placeholder mapping happen before the user sees the verification PDF?
answered.answer: LLM auto-maps on upload (analysis + insertion in one pass), no user intervention

answered.question: How should Jinja placeholders appear in the verification PDF?
answered.answer: Styled with light background (light blue/grey box via DOCX shading before Gotenberg conversion)

answered.question: How should the user select items on the PDF for correction?
answered.answer: Reuse 5.2 selection + LLM infers correction type from chat context, no mode buttons

answered.question: What should 'regenerate' actually do under the hood?
answered.answer: Full pipeline: update DOCX + Gotenberg re-convert to PDF (2-5 sec with spinner)

answered.question: How many rounds of corrections should the user be able to make?
answered.answer: Unlimited + explicit 'Approve & Continue' button, no warnings or limits

answered.question: What happens to Phase 5.2's manual selection-and-describe workflow?
answered.answer: 5.3 replaces 5.2 for Analysis step; 5.2 selection infra reused for corrections

answered.question: How should the LLM process correction requests and return the updated mapping?
answered.answer: Structured JSON response with full updated mapping (same schema as Phase 5)

answered.question: After regeneration, should previous selections persist on the new PDF?
answered.answer: Clear all selections — fresh PDF = fresh slate

answered.question: What should happen when the user clicks 'Approve & Continue'?
answered.answer: Triggers GW data render, Preview step shows rendered result

answered.question: Should the wizard simplify from 5 steps to 4 steps?
answered.answer: Yes: Upload → Verify Placeholders → Preview → Download (merge Analysis + Adaptation)

answered.question: How should the auto-map processing be shown to the user after upload?
answered.answer: Progress steps in Upload step, auto-advance to Verify. Reuse existing progress infra with persistence fixes

answered.question: Should the chat panel be visible by default in the Verify step?
answered.answer: Always visible, same PDF-left/chat-right layout as Phase 5.2

answered.question: What should happen if the LLM's auto-map produces a poor result?
answered.answer: Show it anyway, trust the correction flow. KB learning improves over time

answered.question: How should the coverage counter work in the verification model?
answered.answer: Show X placeholders count in toolbar, KB badge stays (fires on download)

answered.question: How should the document structure browser work in the verification model?
answered.answer: Repurposed as placeholder navigation map — lists all placeholders, click to jump in PDF

answered.question: How should Translation (Phase 6) fit into the new 4-step wizard?
answered.answer: Optional Step 4: Upload → Verify → Preview → [Translate] → Download. Phase 6 re-planned from scratch. Auto-detect EN/PT-PT

answered.question: How should the KB store document zone information?
answered.answer: Add zone column to existing TemplateMapping table (header|footer|body|table_cell|text_box|cover|unknown)

answered.question: How should the KB handle repetition patterns?
answered.answer: Store repetition count per zone per gwField per templateType

answered.question: Should the KB store structural blueprints?
answered.answer: Yes, composite blueprint records (loop opener + inner fields + loop closer, co-occurring markers)

answered.question: How should confidence calibration work on corrections?
answered.answer: Decay on correction (0.7x), boost on confirmation (min(1.0, +0.1))

answered.question: Should the KB use style-based heuristics?
answered.answer: Yes, StyleHint model tracks style-to-zone mappability frequency, filters boilerplate from prompt

answered.question: How should enhanced KB inject context into the LLM prompt?
answered.answer: Structured context block with Zone Distribution + Blueprints + Confidence Notes sections

answered.question: How should the new KB models be structured in Prisma?
answered.answer: Evolve TemplateMapping (add zone, zoneRepetitionCount, correctionCount) + new BlueprintPattern + new StyleHint

answered.question: When should KB entries be populated?
answered.answer: Three triggers: on download (existing) + on correction (new) + on blueprint detection (new)

answered.question: How should the KB handle cross-template-type learning?
answered.answer: Template-type-scoped primary lookup with optional cross-type fallback at 0.7x confidence penalty

answered.question: What should happen on cold start (no KB entries)?
answered.answer: Reference template patterns + structural inference from style heuristics, no seed dataset required

answered.question: How should blueprint patterns be automatically detected?
answered.answer: Heuristic rules based on marker adjacency and type (deterministic, no LLM needed)

answered.question: How much prompt token budget should KB context consume?
answered.answer: Dynamic 15-25% of prompt, capped at 1500 tokens. Priority: blueprints > zone-tagged > flat

answered.question: How should correction input work in the mapping column?
answered.answer: GW field autocomplete that auto-generates Jinja2 syntax, with raw Jinja2 input fallback for bootstrapping

answered.question: Should direct mapping table replace or coexist with LLM correction chat?
answered.answer: Replace entirely — LLM chat lost 70% of corrections in Phase 5.3 testing

answered.question: How should the mapping table be initially populated?
answered.answer: Pre-filled with all auto-mapped entries from LLM; user edits/adds/deletes in place

answered.question: How should removal of wrong mappings work?
answered.answer: Delete row (X button) to remove mapping; on regeneration original text restored

answered.question: How should mapping table relate to PDF view?
answered.answer: Bidirectional sync: click table row → PDF scrolls and highlights; highlight PDF → adds table row. Replaces structure browser

answered.question: Should Phase 5.4 remove LLM from auto-mapping too?
answered.answer: No — LLM stays for auto-map (Pass 1 + Pass 2). KB makes it 80%+. Table is for the remaining 20% corrections

answered.question: Should regeneration process all placeholders or only changed ones?
answered.answer: Full regeneration — LLM processes entire mapping table every time

answered.question: What document representation should the LLM receive?
answered.answer: Structured zone map + paragraph text (zone-aware parser output with numbered paragraphs)

answered.question: Should the LLM produce the final DOCX or return placement instructions?
answered.answer: Placement instructions (structured JSON) — sanitization service validates and applies

answered.question: What should happen when LLM placement instructions don't match document content?
answered.answer: Skip and warn — apply valid instructions, show warnings for failures

answered.question: Should the old mechanical apply-from-mapping be kept as fallback?
answered.answer: Replace entirely — no mechanical fallback, LLM is the only path

answered.question: Should initial auto-map also use the new LLM placement pipeline?
answered.answer: Yes, unify both paths — same pipeline for auto-map and regeneration

answered.question: Should LLM placement decisions enrich the KB with structural context?
answered.answer: Yes — zone, marker type, co-occurrence patterns fed back to KB

inferred.source: persistence timing decision

inferred.requirement: Translation prompt includes target language reference template from reference_loader
inferred.source: reference template decision — PT-PT references already in reference_loader

inferred.requirement: Placeholder token map must be stored in wizard state for validation and restoration
inferred.source: placeholder replacement strategy requires reversible token→Jinja2 mapping

inferred.requirement: Translation step extends wizard from 5 steps to 6 (optional translate between feedback and download)
inferred.source: wizard timing decision

inferred.requirement: Download step must handle two DOCX files when translation is present
inferred.source: both versions downloadable decision

inferred.requirement: Translation model config follows existing per-feature pattern in llm_config
inferred.source: Sonnet 4.5 default, Opus upgrade via config change

inferred.requirement: PdfPreview component must be extended with pdfjs text layer selection and custom event handlers for text highlighting
inferred.source: PDF text selection decision — pdfjs-dist text layer provides selectable spans

inferred.requirement: Structure browser sidebar reads DOCX paragraph data from existing session analysis (Phase 4/5 pipeline)
inferred.source: structure panel decision — reuses parsed document structure

inferred.requirement: Chat prompt must handle free-form numbered references (#1, #2...) and resolve each to a GW field + marker type
inferred.source: batch chat + LLM resolution decisions

inferred.requirement: Mapping overlays use PDF text layer coordinates from selection metadata for positioning
inferred.source: inline confirmation decision — overlays at selection locations

inferred.requirement: Phase 5.1 annotated preview (yellow/green shading) is replaced by green-only confirmed mapping highlights
inferred.source: green-only highlighting decision — no gap shading

inferred.requirement: KB persistence continues to trigger on download (Phase 5.1 pattern) with template type metadata
inferred.source: KB per template type decision — extends existing KB with type filtering

inferred.requirement: Selection state machine: pending (blue) → described → confirmed (green) or rejected (orange) → re-described → confirmed/rejected
inferred.source: rejection flow + visual states decisions — three-state model

inferred.requirement: Chat prompt must support re-mapping: when user references existing numbered selections, LLM updates only those mappings without touching confirmed ones
inferred.source: batch correction decision — LLM re-maps only specified selections

inferred.requirement: Wizard restructured from 5 steps to 4: Upload → Verify Placeholders → Preview → Download. Analysis and Adaptation steps merged into auto-map flow
inferred.source: wizard restructuring decision — 5.3 merges analysis + adaptation

inferred.requirement: LLM auto-maps on upload: Pass 1 (analysis) + Pass 2 (insertion) run without user intervention, progress shown in Upload step
inferred.source: auto-mapping trigger decision — LLM does heavy lifting, user only corrects

inferred.requirement: Verification PDF shows Jinja placeholders with light background styling via DOCX shading (same Gotenberg pipeline as 5.1)
inferred.source: placeholder styling decision — scannable placeholders in long documents

inferred.requirement: Phase 5.2 selection infrastructure (pdfjs text selection, numbered items, batch chat) reused for correction flow, but manual mapping workflow retired
inferred.source: 5.2 → 5.3 transition decision — auto-map replaces manual mapping

inferred.requirement: LLM correction prompt receives current mapping + user corrections, returns full updated mapping as structured JSON (same schema as Phase 5)
inferred.source: correction response format decision — predictable, validatable output

inferred.requirement: Full pipeline regeneration on corrections: DOCX re-insertion + Gotenberg PDF conversion. Selections cleared after each regeneration
inferred.source: regeneration + selection reset decisions — always accurate, fresh slate

inferred.requirement: Document structure browser repurposed as placeholder navigation map — lists all placeholders with click-to-jump. Essential for long docs and hidden sections
inferred.source: structure browser repurpose decision — navigation over selection

inferred.requirement: Auto-map progress must reuse existing progress infrastructure with state persistence fixes (no reset on refresh/navigation)
inferred.source: processing UX decision — user explicitly called out refresh bug fixes must carry forward

inferred.requirement: Translation (Phase 6) will insert as optional Step 4 between Preview and Download. Phase 6 re-planned from scratch for new wizard structure
inferred.source: translation integration decision — old Phase 6 planning stale

inferred.requirement: Phase 6 old planning artifacts are stale and should be cleaned up; Phase 6 will be re-discussed and re-planned from scratch
inferred.source: user stated old Phase 6 was planned before 5.1/5.2/5.3 existed

inferred.requirement: TemplateMapping gets zone, zoneRepetitionCount, correctionCount fields; existing entries migrate with zone='unknown'
inferred.source: zone storage + repetition tracking decisions

inferred.requirement: New BlueprintPattern Prisma model stores composite structural patterns (loop, conditional, group) as ordered marker arrays
inferred.source: structural blueprints decision — multi-marker patterns always appear as coordinated groups

inferred.requirement: New StyleHint Prisma model tracks style-to-zone mappability frequency for boilerplate filtering
inferred.source: style heuristics decision — 60%+ prompt noise reduction

inferred.requirement: Confidence decay (0.7x) on correction and boost (+0.1, cap 1.0) on confirmation replace usageCount-only approach
inferred.source: confidence calibration decision — prevents wrong mappings from poisoning prompts

inferred.requirement: Analysis prompt replaces flat few-shot list with structured context block (Zone Distribution + Blueprints + Confidence Notes)
inferred.source: prompt format decision — structured context is more actionable for LLM

inferred.requirement: Blueprint detection uses heuristic rules (marker adjacency, prefix grouping, control-flow bracketing) not LLM
inferred.source: blueprint detection decision — deterministic patterns need no LLM tokens

inferred.requirement: KB context dynamically sized to 15-25% of prompt, capped at 1500 tokens, priority: blueprints > zone-tagged > flat
inferred.source: token budget decision — balance KB context vs document structure

inferred.requirement: Cross-type fallback at 0.7x confidence penalty bootstraps new template types from existing KB data
inferred.source: cross-type learning decision — most mappings are shared across types

inferred.requirement: Cold start uses reference template patterns + style-based zone inference; no seed dataset required
inferred.source: cold start decision — reference templates already provide good structural patterns

inferred.requirement: Correction flow (Phase 5.3) triggers immediate KB confidence update (decay old, boost new) instead of waiting for download
inferred.source: population trigger decision — corrections are highest-signal feedback

inferred.requirement: LLM correction chat removed from Step 2 (Verify Placeholders). All corrections through direct mapping table editing
inferred.source: user-initiated UX redesign — LLM chat lost 70% of corrections in 5.3 test

inferred.requirement: Mapping table pre-filled with all auto-mapped entries. Each row: index, document text, GW field/Jinja2 expression. Supports add (via PDF highlight), edit (inline), delete (remove mapping)
inferred.source: direct mapping table decision — table IS the mapping plan

inferred.requirement: GW field autocomplete dropdown generates correct Jinja2 syntax from field type + zone. Raw Jinja2 input also supported for bootstrapping and advanced use
inferred.source: autocomplete decision — users shouldn't need to know Jinja2 syntax

inferred.requirement: Bidirectional table-PDF sync replaces structure browser. Click row → PDF scrolls and highlights. Highlight PDF text → new table row added
inferred.source: table-PDF sync decision — table becomes both the mapping editor and the navigation tool

inferred.requirement: Delete row = remove mapping. Regeneration restores original text at that location. No blank-field ambiguity
inferred.source: removal flow decision — delete is unambiguous vs empty field

inferred.requirement: LLM receives structured zone map (header/footer/body/table zones with numbered paragraphs and text) instead of raw DOCX XML
inferred.source: document representation decision — zone-aware parser output balances structure vs token cost

inferred.requirement: LLM returns structured JSON placement instructions (paragraph index + original text + replacement placeholder). Sanitization service validates substring match before applying
inferred.source: LLM output format decision — validate before touching document

inferred.requirement: Full regeneration: every click processes entire mapping table. No incremental diffing or change tracking
inferred.source: regeneration scope decision — LLM needs full picture for correct placement

inferred.requirement: Unified pipeline: initial auto-map (on upload) and user-triggered regeneration use identical LLM placement code
inferred.source: pipeline unification decision — one code path, consistent behavior

inferred.requirement: Old mechanical apply-from-mapping text-replacement logic fully removed. No fallback path
inferred.source: replace entirely decision — old approach fundamentally broken for user-added entries

inferred.requirement: Skip-and-warn: mismatched instructions skipped, remaining applied, user sees warnings for failed placements
inferred.source: error handling decision — graceful degradation with user visibility

inferred.requirement: KB enrichment: LLM placement decisions store zone + marker type context back to KB for future auto-map improvement
inferred.source: KB enrichment decision — structural context improves accuracy over time

inferred.requirement: Remove .mapped-highlight CSS and related PDF text layer highlighting code (blue highlights doing nothing)
inferred.source: cleanup — unused visual noise from previous commit

inferred.requirement: KB lookup uses (normalizedSectionText + zone + templateType + language) as match key with zone in unique constraint
inferred.source: match key decision — zone prevents cross-zone collisions

inferred.requirement: Exact normalized match only, no fuzzy or embedding-based matching. Templates are structurally stable across reuse
inferred.source: matching strategy decision — 3-5 templates reused dozens of times

inferred.requirement: Locked sections and unknowns presented as inline annotations in document order: [RESOLVED: field] / [NEEDS MAPPING]. LLM sees positional context
inferred.source: prompt framing decision — document order matters for LLM to infer unknowns from surrounding sections

inferred.requirement: LLM receives batch of unknown sections with locked mappings as hard constraints. Prompt: 'these sections are already mapped (do not change them), now map the remaining ones'
inferred.source: LLM fallback decision — fewer unknowns + full structural context = better results per LLM call

inferred.requirement: Corrections use delete-and-recreate: delete wrong entry, create correct one at 1.0. No confidence decay, no ghost entries
inferred.source: correction model decision — user correction is unambiguous ground truth

inferred.requirement: Lock threshold >= 0.8, new entries start at 1.0 (lock immediately on re-upload)
inferred.source: threshold decision — first correct mapping locks immediately, pragmatic for reuse-heavy scenario

inferred.requirement: One-time migration prunes entries below confidence 0.3 with log. Ongoing auto-prune during persistMappingsToKB()
inferred.source: pruning decision — one-time cleanup + self-cleaning going forward

inferred.requirement: KB badge + muted row styling via source: 'kb' | 'llm' field. Purely CSS, no functional changes to mapping table data flow, events, or PDF pipeline
inferred.source: UI decision — cosmetic layer only, zero functional changes to existing behavior

inferred.requirement: Summary banner above mapping table: 'X/Y sections resolved from KB, Z analysed by LLM'
inferred.source: UX decision — quick confidence signal that KB is working

inferred.requirement: Backend does KB lookup via Prisma, splits sections into locked/unknown arrays, passes both to Python for prompt building
inferred.source: architecture decision — clean separation of data logic (Node) and prompt logic (Python)

inferred.requirement: Remove cross-type fallback code entirely. First upload of new template type = full LLM. Second upload = fully locked
inferred.source: cold start decision — cross-type is dead weight with prescriptive model

inferred.requirement: Repeating sections (findings loop): match by text+zone, apply to all occurrences
inferred.source: repetition decision — all 'Description' entries in body zone map to same field

inferred.requirement: Full reconciliation on download: diff final plan vs locked entries. Unchanged = usageCount++. Changed = delete+recreate. New = create at 1.0
inferred.source: persistence decision — closes the feedback loop with delete+recreate consistency

inferred.requirement: Add zone to TemplateMapping unique constraint. Backfill NULL zones from blueprint patterns before migration. Log all changes
inferred.source: schema decision — zone is part of match key, constraint must reflect that

inferred.requirement: Keep blueprints section in prompt for structural patterns. Remove zone distribution, repetition hints, confidence notes, token budget enforcer
inferred.source: prompt cleanup decision — inline annotations replace old KB context block

inferred.requirement: Template fingerprinting deferred to future phase. Per-section prescriptive lookup sufficient for current use case
inferred.source: scope decision — fingerprinting is optimization on top, not needed yet
